channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Philippine Police Reports Decrease in Key Crimes Nationwide
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) reported a 12-percent reduction in focus crimes across the country from April 6 to May 6, 2026. According to PNP data, total crimes dropped from 3,047 to 2,684, re...

item.title: A Detailed Look at Worship Practices Across Nigeria
item.description: For anybody wey dey try understand how Nigerians dey worship, e no be one-size-fits-all matter. Nigeria get more than 200 million people and over 250 ethnic groups, so worship dey vary sharply across ...

item.title: Ramaphosa to Challenge Section 89 Panel Report Following Constitutional Court Ruling
item.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa addressed the nation on Monday night, firmly stating that he has not committed any crimes nor misused public funds. He insisted there is insufficient evidence to suggest he v...

item.title: Sweden Shifts Tanzania Partnership from Aid to Trade and Infrastructure Financing
item.description: Sweden is changing its long-standing relationship with Tanzania, moving from over six decades of development aid to a partnership focused on trade, investment, and export finance. Swedish Ambassador C...

item.title: The Prado’s Mona Lisa: A Contemporary Studio Version of Leonardo’s Masterpiece
item.description: The so-called “Prado Mona Lisa,” housed in Madrid’s Museo del Prado since 1819, has undergone a major reevaluation in recent years. Long dismissed as a later and relatively unimportant copy of Leonard...

item.title: Recent Attacks in Mali Challenge Russian-backed Security Support
item.description: In Mali, the security situation has taken a sharp turn as coordinated attacks by the Jama’at Nusrat al-Islam wal-Muslimin (JNIM) and the Front de Libération de l’Azawad (FLA) exposed the limits of Rus...

item.title: Posthumous memoir by Virginia Giuffre named Book of the Year at British Book Awards
item.description: A posthumous memoir by Virginia Giuffre, one of the most prominent accusers of Jeffrey Epstein, has been awarded Book of the Year at the British Book Awards in London. Titled Nobody’s Girl and co-writ...

item.title: Fat Dog release new '80s-inspired single ‘Go Fuck Urself’ ahead of extensive 2026 tour
item.description: South London band Fat Dog have returned with their latest single ‘Go Fuck Urself’, their first original music release of 2026. Following the success of their 2024 debut album 'WOOF.' and last summer's...

item.title: PNPA Expels 42 Cadets for Remaining Silent on Hazing Incidents
item.description: Forty-two cadets at the Philippine National Police Academy (PNPA) were expelled after failing to report a hazing incident that caused serious injuries to 22 plebes. The plebes suffered burns and other...

item.title: Supreme Court clears way for Alabama to use contested congressional map affecting majority-Black district
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court has allowed Alabama to move forward with a congressional map that reduces the number of majority-Black districts to one. The ruling came quickly and without explanation, overtur...

item.title: B.C. Health Officer Confirms Evacuated Canadians from Hantavirus Cruise Show No Symptoms
item.description: British Columbia’s Provincial Health Officer, Dr. Bonnie Henry, has confirmed that the four Canadians repatriated from the hantavirus-affected MV Hondius cruise ship over the weekend have had no known...

item.title: Exploring Silence and Loss Through Poetry: An Excerpt from Naveen Kishore
item.description: The article presents an excerpt from Naveen Kishore's poetry collection 'Ode to a Grieving Angel', focusing on the theme of inexpressible grief and the limits of language. The piece opens with a theat...

item.title: Queensland Parliament Focuses on Federal Budget, Stafford Byelection, and CFMEU Inquiry
item.description: This live blog entry from Queensland Parliament sets the scene for a politically significant day, even if legislative activity itself is expected to be relatively quiet. Attention is largely centred o...

item.title: Artisanal gold miners return to dangerous pits in Elgeyo Marakwet hours after collapse
item.description: Gold miners in Elgeyo Marakwet County have gone back to work in the same artisanal mining pits where a deadly collapse happened just one day earlier. The return to the site comes despite the ongoing r...

item.title: Niger State Supports Tinubu’s Vision for Second Term, Says Hope Ambassadors
item.description: The Renewed Hope Ambassadors in Niger State don talk say the entire North dey support President Bola Tinubu as e dey pursue second term. Nma Kolo, wey be state coordinator, yan for media chat say the ...

item.title: Malolos City Policeman Placed Under Investigation for Domestic Violence
item.description: A police officer in Malolos City, Bulacan, has been relieved from his post, disarmed, and placed under restrictive custody following reports and video evidence of domestic abuse. The incident came to ...

item.title: U.S. Army Recovers Body of Soldier Missing in Morocco During Training
item.description: The U.S. Army confirmed the recovery of First Lieutenant Kendrick Lamont Key Jr.'s body, who went missing in Morocco while participating in an annual training exercise. Key, 27, and another U.S. servi...

item.title: Expert Criticizes FBI Director Kash Patel's Leadership and Use of Polygraph Tests
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel has drawn sharp criticism from former DOJ prosecutor Glenn Kirschner over his recent directive for FBI staff to undergo polygraph tests concerning agency leaks. Kirschner warne...

item.title: Modi embarks on international tour to strengthen trade and strategic ties
item.description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is set to begin a week-long tour covering the United Arab Emirates and four European nations, officials confirmed on Monday. The trip will start in the UAE on Frida...

item.title: VP Sara Duterte says ICC arrest warrant for Bato dela Rosa should go through Philippine courts first
item.description: Vice President Sara Duterte criticized the attempt to serve an International Criminal Court (ICC) arrest warrant against Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa, emphasizing that foreign-issued warrants must ...
